_doc: Run Promotion Policy — governs when to escalate from cheaper to more expensive profiles.

static_validation.description: Run CompileHygiene, CompletionVerifier, or StubGen without an LLM run.
static_validation.answers: Are there new blocking issues? Did a prior fix work? Is the baseline still clean?

dev.description: Quick iteration, architecture discussion, lightweight code generation.
dev.answers: Does the pipeline run end-to-end? Do new agents/passes work? Is the Ops Layer stable?
preconditions: static_validation shows 0 BLOCKING or a specific question needs LLM

standard.description: Full code generation, quality reviews, fix execution. Production-grade output.
standard.answers: Does the factory produce compilable, well-structured code under realistic load?
preconditions: dev runs show the pipeline is stable (MOSTLY_COMPLETE)
a specific code-quality or generation-volume question cannot be answered with dev
static validation confirms 0 BLOCKING on current baseline

premium.description: Maximum quality. Complex architecture decisions, deep refactoring.
premium.answers: Is the architecture fundamentally sound? Are there subtle design issues?
preconditions: standard runs produce compilable output but quality/architecture needs improvement
a specific deep-reasoning question cannot be answered with standard
budget explicitly approved for premium run

promotion_rules.static_to_dev: Promote when static validation cannot answer the current question (e.g., need to test new agent behavior, pipeline flow, or code extraction).
promotion_rules.dev_to_standard: Promote when dev runs are stable (MOSTLY_COMPLETE) but code output is insufficient (too few files, Haiku limits reached, or code quality question).
promotion_rules.standard_to_premium: Promote only when standard output is compilable but architecture/design quality needs expert review. Requires explicit budget approval.
promotion_rules.any_to_static: Demote when the current question can be answered by running CompileHygiene, CompletionVerifier, or reading existing reports — no LLM needed.

current_state_assessment.reason: Sonnet run proved the pipeline works under load. No open question requires another LLM run. Next step is static validation after ClosedRange fix, or a different feature template to test breadth.
